relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Trust in the glorious Lord cannot coexist with favoritism.
The rich and poor enter the same gathering.
Honor given by appearance exposes the heart.
Favoritism makes harmful judges.
The poor are chosen rich in trust and heirs of the reign.
Dishonoring the poor contradicts Divine choosing.
The name over the community is blasphemed by oppressive power.
The royal Law is neighbor-love.
Favoritism exposes misalignment.
The Law is whole and cannot be sliced for convenience.
The same Lawgiver speaks against multiple forms of harm.
Speak and act as those under the Law of freedom.
Mercy triumphs over judgment.
Trust without embodied benefit cannot heal.
A sibling lacking clothing and food reveals the test.
Words of peace without bodily care are empty.
Trust without works is dead by itself.
Works make trust visible.
Even correct belief can tremble without love.
Inactive trust is exposed as empty.
Abraham’s trust moved through action.
Trust and works worked together into completion.
Abraham trusted and was called friend of the Divine.
Righteousness/justice is shown through embodied trust.
Rahab’s welcome and action are honored.
As body without breath is dead, trust without works is dead.

contemplative_note: James 2 is faith made visible: no favoritism, mercy over judgment, and trust becoming food, clothing, welcome, and justice.
